title: Create DB migration: AFTER INSERT trigger on agent_versions to stamp promoted_version
spec:
Create a Supabase migration file that adds an AFTER INSERT trigger on the agent_versions table.

Trigger logic:
- Fires AFTER INSERT on agent_versions
- When NEW.env = 'production', execute:
  UPDATE features
  SET promoted_version = NEW.version
  WHERE status = 'complete'
    AND promoted_version IS NULL
    AND company_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001';

Notes:
- agent_versions has no company_id column — hardcode the zazig company UUID in the WHERE clause
- The trigger runs as the table owner (bypasses RLS) so no policy changes needed
- The WHERE clause makes it idempotent: only unpromoted complete features are touched
- Follow the existing migration file naming convention in the supabase/migrations directory
acceptance_tests:
Given a migration file exists in supabase/migrations for the agent_versions trigger
When the migration is applied to the database
Then a trigger named appropriately exists on agent_versions that fires AFTER INSERT
And when a row with env='production' is inserted into agent_versions
Then features with status='complete' and promoted_version IS NULL and company_id='00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001' have promoted_version set to NEW.version
And features with promoted_version IS NOT NULL are not modified
And features with status != 'complete' are not modified

title: Remove bulk promoted_version update block from promote.ts
spec:
Remove the direct Supabase .update() call from packages/cli/src/commands/promote.ts that manually stamps promoted_version on features. This logic is now handled by the DB trigger added in the companion migration job.

Specifically:
- Locate the bulk update block around lines 521-535 that sets promoted_version on features where status='complete' and promoted_version IS NULL
- Delete that entire block along with its surrounding try/catch and any associated log lines
- Ensure promote still completes successfully end-to-end after the removal
- Do not remove any other logic from promote.ts
acceptance_tests:
Given the direct Supabase .update() call for promoted_version has been removed from promote.ts
When running zazig promote
Then the command completes successfully without errors
And the removed block (bulk update of promoted_version on features) no longer exists in promote.ts
And no TypeScript compilation errors are introduced
